name: Filo Pastries with Smoked Turkey and Mushrooms
recipeIngredient: 1/4 pound finely minced smoked turkey or ham, trimmed of fat
recipeInstructions:
1. In a large skillet over medium heat, sauté mushrooms, garlic, and onion in sherry and olive oil until soft. Add parsley, feta, mozzarella, oregano, thyme, and turkey. Remove from heat.
2. Preheat oven to 350°F. Lightly oil two baking sheets measuring 9 by 12 inches.
3. Lay filo dough on a clean, dry surface. Cut each sheet in half widthwise to make 30 smaller sheets. Stack them evenly on top of each other, and cover with a piece of plastic wrap and a slightly dampened dish towel as you do the next step.
4. Butter one sheet and cut into three long strips. Lay the three strips on top of each other. Place about two tablespoons of filling in the lower right section near the edge. Fold bottom right corner of filo over filling to meet left edge, creating a small triangle. Continue folding pastry, as you would a flag, until you reach the top. You will end up with a triangle-shaped pastry.
5. Lightly butter the top of filled triangle and place on prepared baking sheet. Repeat process until all the filling has been used.
6. Bake pastries in oven for 20 minutes or until golden. Serve warm.
Makes 30 pastries.
